commands.review_summary:
The PR adds global and per-agent memory role slots for recall, compaction, capture, dreaming, and user modeling, then wires those slots through config, plugin activation, runtime memory resolution, doctor/status flows, docs, and tests while retaining the legacy memory slot alias.

PR surface: Source +1111, Tests +1323, Docs +22, Generated 0. Total +2456 across 77 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/config architecture PR rather than a current-main bug reproduction. The branch does include after-fix real environment proof for the intended role-slot behavior.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of a broad compatibility-sensitive memory/plugin config contract plus conflict refresh, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: memory plugins no longer have to globally displace each other to participate in distinct memory roles. OpenClaw now understands role-slot selections globally and per agent, keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` recall compatibility, preserves global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` selection, and upgrades old memory-slot configs into the new role-slot shape without breaking the legacy alias.
- Real environment tested: Orion/Nano-01 at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210`, branch `feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`, commit `36b01413e8545ba0788ad3b7306538c31ba4bfb4`, Node `v24.15.0`, built from the checked-out PR source. The upgrade proof started from upstream `v2026.5.20` in `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-upgrade-proof-v20260520`. The Honcho proof used Docker on Orion with PostgreSQL/pgvector, Redis, Honcho API, Honcho Deriver, and an OpenAI-compatible OpenClaw gateway backend using `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`. The LosslessClaw proof used real `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2` installed on Orion.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
36b01413e8545ba0788ad3b7306538c31ba4bfb4

$ PATH="$PWD/.artifacts/bin:$PATH" pnpm build:strict-smoke
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.

$ node openclaw.mjs config validate
Config valid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json

$ node openclaw.mjs models status --probe --json
github-copilot/gpt-5-mini probe returned status: ok from profile auth

$ node openclaw.mjs infer model run --local --model github-copilot/gpt-5-mini --prompt 'Reply exactly: OPENCLAW_COPILOT_CURRENT_HEAD_OK' --json
returned OPENCLAW_COPILOT_CURRENT_HEAD_OK

$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$PWD/.artifacts/live-proof-20260525/live-model-config.json" \
  OPENCLAW_LIVE_MODELS="github-copilot/gpt-5-mini" \
  OPENCLAW_LIVE_MODEL_TIMEOUT_MS=90000 \
  OPENCLAW_LIVE_MAX_MODELS=1 \
  PATH="$PWD/.artifacts/bin:$PATH" \
  node scripts/test-live.mjs src/agents/models.profiles.live.test.ts

$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$PWD/.artifacts/live-proof-20260525/live-model-config.json" \
  OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_MODELS="github-copilot/gpt-5-mini" \
  OPENCLAW_LIVE_MODEL_TIMEOUT_MS=90000 \
  OPENCLAW_LIVE_MAX_MODELS=1 \
  PATH="$PWD/.artifacts/bin:$PATH" \
  node scripts/test-live.mjs src/gateway/gateway-models.profiles.live.test.ts

$ cd /home/kklouzal/openclaw-upgrade-proof-v20260520
$ PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" pnpm build:strict-smoke
OpenClaw 2026.5.20 (e510042)
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.

$ node openclaw.mjs config validate
OpenClaw 2026.5.20 (e510042)
Config valid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json

$ cd /home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210
$ node openclaw.mjs doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
$ node openclaw.mjs config validate
Config valid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json

$ node openclaw.mjs plugins install @martian-engineering/lossless-claw --pin --force
Installed plugin: lossless-claw
Exclusive slot "contextEngine" switched from "legacy" to "lossless-claw".

$ OPENCLAW_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS=180000 node openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-key agent:main:lossless-proof --message "/lossless" --json --timeout 180
lossless-claw loaded, initialized ~/.openclaw/lcm.db, and exposed lcm_grep/lcm_describe/lcm_expand/lcm_expand_query tools to the embedded runtime.

$ sudo apt-get install -y docker.io docker-compose docker-buildx
$ sudo nvidia-ctk runtime configure --runtime=docker
$ sudo systemctl restart docker
$ sudo docker run --rm --runtime=nvidia ubuntu:22.04 sh -c 'echo NVIDIA_RUNTIME_CONTAINER_OK'
NVIDIA_RUNTIME_CONTAINER_OK

$ sudo DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=1 docker-compose -p honcho-orion-proof up -d --build
$ curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:8000/health
{"status":"ok"}

$ node openclaw.mjs honcho status
Connected to Honcho
  Workspace: openclaw-pr-86210-orion-honcho-proof
  Default agent: main -> peer "agent-main"
  Agent peers mapped: main

$ node openclaw.mjs honcho ask "For proof, reply with exactly ORION_SELF_HOSTED_HONCHO_OK."
ORION_SELF_HOSTED_HONCHO_OK
```

- Evidence after fix:

Copied live output and redacted runtime logs from Orion/Nano-01 after this patch:

```text
$ node openclaw.mjs infer model run --local --model github-copilot/gpt-5-mini --prompt 'Reply exactly: OPENCLAW_COPILOT_CURRENT_HEAD_OK' --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "model.run",
  "transport": "local",
  "provider": "github-copilot",
  "model": "gpt-5-mini",
  "outputs": [
    { "text": "OPENCLAW_COPILOT_CURRENT_HEAD_OK", "mediaUrl": null }
  ]
}

[live] [live-models] 1/1 github-copilot/gpt-5-mini: prompt
[live] [live-models] 1/1 github-copilot/gpt-5-mini: file-read probe
[live] [live-models] 1/1 github-copilot/gpt-5-mini: image probe
[live] [live-models] 1/1 github-copilot/gpt-5-mini: done
✓ src/agents/models.profiles.live.test.ts (17 tests)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)

[live] [all-models] 1/1 github-copilot/gpt-5-mini: prompt
[live] [all-models] 1/1 github-copilot/gpt-5-mini: tool-read
[live] [all-models] 1/1 github-copilot/gpt-5-mini: tool-exec
✓ src/gateway/gateway-models.profiles.live.test.ts (54 tests)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  54 passed (54)

Doctor changes:
- plugins.slots.memory: copied legacy memory slot to memory.recall (memory-core) and kept the legacy key for plugin compatibility.

Doctor warnings:
- plugins.slots.memory: legacy memory slot remains for plugin compatibility; use memory.recall as the canonical selector.

After doctor --fix:
{
  "slots": {
    "memory": "memory-core",
    "memory.recall": "memory-core"
  },
  "entries": ["github-copilot", "memory-core"],
  "agents": [{"id":"main"}]
}

LosslessClaw install/runtime:
Pinned npm install record to @martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2.
Exclusive slot "contextEngine" switched from "legacy" to "lossless-claw".
[plugins] [lcm] Engine initialized for db=/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/lcm.db
[plugins] [lcm] Plugin loaded (enabled=true, db=/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/lcm.db, threshold=0.75, proactiveThresholdCompactionMode=deferred)
Tools exposed: lcm_grep, lcm_describe, lcm_expand, lcm_expand_query

Docker/NVIDIA:
Docker version 29.1.3
Docker Compose v1.29.2
Buildx 0.30.1
Docker runtimes include nvidia: nvidia-container-runtime
NVIDIA_RUNTIME_CONTAINER_OK

Honcho self-hosted on Orion:
honcho-orion-proof_api_1        Up (healthy)   127.0.0.1:8000->8000/tcp
honcho-orion-proof_database_1   Up (healthy)   127.0.0.1:5432->5432/tcp
honcho-orion-proof_redis_1      Up (healthy)   127.0.0.1:6379->6379/tcp
{"status":"ok"}

$ node openclaw.mjs honcho status
[plugins] Honcho memory plugin loaded
Connected to Honcho
  Workspace: openclaw-pr-86210-orion-honcho-proof
  Default agent: main -> peer "agent-main"
  Agent peers mapped: main

$ node openclaw.mjs honcho ask "For proof, reply with exactly ORION_SELF_HOSTED_HONCHO_OK."
[plugins] Honcho memory plugin loaded
ORION_SELF_HOSTED_HONCHO_OK
```

Resolver proof from `.artifacts/final-proof-20260525/final-proof-summary.json` confirmed the final configured slot spread:

```text
plugins.slots.memory.recall = memory-core
plugins.slots.memory.dreaming = memory-core
plugins.slots.memory.userModel = openclaw-honcho
plugins.slots.contextEngine = lossless-claw
openclaw-honcho.baseUrl = http://localhost:8000
openclaw-honcho.workspaceId = openclaw-pr-86210-orion-honcho-proof
openclaw-honcho.hasApiKey = false
```

- Observed result after fix: current-head OpenClaw built successfully, validated the runtime config, used the real GitHub Copilot profile for `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`, completed direct local inference, completed the live direct model prompt/file-read/image probes, completed the live gateway prompt/tool-read/tool-exec path, upgraded a real `v2026.5.20` legacy `plugins.slots.memory` config by copying it to `memory.recall` while retaining the legacy key, installed and loaded real LosslessClaw as the global `contextEngine`, initialized the LosslessClaw SQLite database and exposed LCM tools, installed Docker/Compose/Buildx with NVIDIA runtime support on Orion, started a self-hosted Honcho stack on Orion, connected OpenClaw to that local Honcho workspace without `HONCHO_API_KEY`, and returned the exact `ORION_SELF_HOSTED_HONCHO_OK` marker through `openclaw honcho ask`.
- What was not tested: hosted `api.honcho.dev` was not used for the final proof because the final Honcho proof is self-hosted on Orion. LosslessClaw summary generation was not forced; the proof covers real install, slot selection, database initialization, embedded runtime tool exposure, and startup maintenance. Future owner-provenance object semantics from #70823 are not implemented or tested here.


## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

Current pushed head:

- `36b01413e8545ba0788ad3b7306538c31ba4bfb4` (`fix(hooks): include agent memory role slots`)

Current-head CI/checks:

- `CI` run `26384675632`: passed broad Node, Android, macOS, docs, lint, type, dependency, and workflow shards.
- `CodeQL Critical Quality` run `26384675611`: passed selected critical runtime/package boundary shards, including agent runtime, config, gateway runtime, memory runtime, plugin boundary, plugin SDK package contract, provider runtime, network runtime.
- `Security High` run `26384675592`: passed actions, channel runtime, core auth/secrets, MCP process tool boundary, network SSRF boundary, and plugin trust boundary shards.
- `OpenGrep — PR Diff`: passed.
- `Real behavior proof`: passed.
- `Socket Security`: project report and PR alerts passed.

Remote live proof on Orion/Nano-01 from current source:

- `PATH="$PWD/.artifacts/bin:$PATH" pnpm build:strict-smoke` -> passed.
- `node openclaw.mjs config validate` -> passed.
- `node openclaw.mjs models status --probe --json` -> `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini` profile probe `status: ok`.
- `node openclaw.mjs infer model run --local --model github-copilot/gpt-5-mini --prompt 'Reply exactly: OPENCLAW_COPILOT_CURRENT_HEAD_OK' --json` -> returned exact marker.
- `node scripts/test-live.mjs src/agents/models.profiles.live.test.ts` with `OPENCLAW_LIVE_MODELS=github-copilot/gpt-5-mini` -> 17 tests passed; prompt, file-read, and image probe completed.
- `node scripts/test-live.mjs src/gateway/gateway-models.profiles.live.test.ts` with `OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_MODELS=github-copilot/gpt-5-mini` -> 54 tests passed; prompt, tool-read, and tool-exec stages completed before the configured model timeout/skip accounting.

Focused local verification previously run on this branch:

- `corepack pnpm exec tsx src/entry.ts config schema | jq '.properties.plugins.properties.slots.properties | keys'`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-memory-slot.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/memory-state.test.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.extensions.test.ts src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/plugin-hooks.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` on touched files.
- `git diff --check`

## Reviewer focus

- Whether the durable memory role names are the right public API: `recall`, `compaction`, `capture`, `dreaming`, and `userModel`.
- Whether keeping legacy `plugins.slots.memory` in place during doctor migration is the right compatibility window.
- Whether per-agent plugin slots should remain memory-only for this PR.
- Whether the linked related issues are accurately classified as closed, related, partially addressed, or future work.
